data.title: H&R Block Appoints Richard Johnson as Chairman
data.description:
H&R Block said Wednesday that its board has elected Richard Johnson as its new chairman, effective Tuesday.

Johnson, a board member since 2015, succeeds Robert Gerard, who has held the chairman position since 2011, the company said.

data.title: Stock markets retreat as reciprocal tariffs sting
data.description:
In adddtion to 10% blanket tariff, Trump has announced "half" tariffs on countries in reaction to tariffs on US goods.

Below is the chart of some of the tariffs. He went big.

If the products are made the US there will not be any tariffs.

Shares which were up have turned lower with the QQQ now down -3.02%. The SPY is down -2.19%

The EURUSD is back down testing the 100 and 200 bar MA on the 4-hour chart at 1.0809 down to 1.0802. So far, that is stalling the fall.

The USDCAD fell below the 100-day MA and 38.2% target, around 1.4269, but then snapped back higher and traded above the 100/200 bar MAs on the 4-hour chart between 1.4325 to 1.4339 The high reached 1.4366 back has moved back down. Seller had their shot. Buyers had their shot. The price is not at 1.4316 waiting for the next shove.

Yields are lower with the 2 year down -1.5 basis point. The 10 year is down -3.0 basis points.

data.title: Dollar moves higher against the yen as Trump announces tariffs
data.description:
The dollar weakened against the euro and turned higher against the yen on Wednesday as U.S. President Donald Trump began a long awaited announcement of sweeping new tariffs that look certain to escalate a trade war with global partners, increase prices and upend a decades-old trade order.

In late trade after Trump reiterated the United States would impose 25% tariffs on foreign-made autos, the euro extended a rise to $1.0895, up 0.92%, while the dollar turned 0.38% higher against the yen to 150.20 yen.

data.title: Report: Chinese Firms Ordered $16 Billion in Nvidia Chips in Q1
data.description:
More than $16 billion in orders for Nvidia's (NVDA, Financials) H20 server chips have been placed by Chinese firms during the first quarter of 2025, according to a report by The Information citing individuals familiar with the matter.

The buyers include some of China's most prominent technology companies: ByteDance, Alibaba Group (BABA, Financials), and Tencent Holdings. These chips represent the most advanced artificial intelligence processors that Nvidia is legally permitted to sell in China under current U.S. export controls, which were tightened in October 2023.

The surge in orders is reportedly linked to demand from DeepSeek, a Chinese artificial intelligence startup building cost-efficient models for the local market. Nvidia, as well as the involved Chinese firms, did not respond to requests for comment, Reuters reported.

H3C, one of China's largest server manufacturers and a key Nvidia OEM partner, raised concerns in March about possible shortages of the H20 chip. Other original equipment manufacturers working with Nvidia in China include Inspur, Lenovo, and xFusion, the x86 server arm spun off from Huawei.

The H20 chip was introduced following U.S. export restrictions implemented in 2023. These controls followed broader limitations initiated in 2022 that aimed to curb China's access to advanced semiconductor technologies, with U.S. officials citing concerns about potential military applications.

In February, former U.S. President Donald Trump said he would introduce tariffs of about 25% on semiconductor imports if re-elected. Nvidia Chief Executive Jensen Huang said at the time that while the company expected little short-term impact, it may eventually consider moving production to the United States.

Despite ongoing trade tensions and restrictions, China remains a key market for Nvidia. The company reported revenue of $17.11 billion from the region, including Hong Kong, in its fiscal 2025 results.

data.title: RH 4Q Revenue Misses Guidance, Looks to Offset Reciprocal TariffsUpdate
data.description:
By Sabela Ojea

RH reported fourth-quarter revenue that missed both guidance and Wall Street expectations, and said it will work to mitigate the impact of reciprocal tariffs on imports.

Shares of RH dropped 25% to $187 in post-market trading following the results. Through Wednesday's close, the stock is down 36% year to date.

The Corte Madera, Calif. furniture retailer on Wednesday reported net income for the three-months ended Feb. 1 of $13.9 million, or 69 cents a share, compared with $11.4 million, or 57 cents a share, for the same period a year earlier.

Stripping out one-time items, the company's earnings per share came in at $1.58. Analysts polled by FactSet had forecast adjusted earnings of $1.92 a share.

Revenue rose 10% to $812.4 million amid a 17% increase in total demand, missing the $829.6 million forecast by Wall Street. The company had most recently guided for a rise in revenue of 18% to 20% from the same period a year earlier amid views for a rise in demand of 20% to 22%.

The company is in the midst of turning around the business to bring out what it called a more inspiring and disruptive RH brand while facing higher tariffs on imported goods.

For the fiscal first quarter, RH guided for revenue growth of 16% and full-year growth of about 15%. Wall Street expected first-quarter revenue to rise 16% and full-year revenue growth of around 15%.

RH doesn't expect a negative impact on results related to previously disclosed increases in tariffs on products from China, Canada or Mexico, Chief Executive Gary Friedman said.

"As it relates to reciprocal and other tariffs that will be announced today, as we've done with prior tariffs, we will be working with our manufacturing partners to mitigate the impact to both our margins and costs to our customers," Friedman said.

Soon after RH reported results President Trump disclosed his plans for reciprocal tariffs. The U.S. will impose a 10%, across-the-board tariff on all imports, and higher rates on other nations the White House considers bad actors on trade.

Write to Sabela Ojea at sabela.ojea@wsj.com; @sabelaojeaguix

data.title: Kaiser Aluminum Changes Method From Last-In, First-Out to Weighted Avg Cost Effective Jan. 1, 2025
UFC Signs Multiyear Partnership With Meta to Expand Global Fan Engagement
data.description:
UFC, the mixed martial arts organization owned by TKO Group Holdings (TKO, Financials), said it signed a multiyear deal with Meta Platforms to integrate technologies from across Meta's (META, Financials) ecosystem into UFC's fan experiences and event coverage.

According to the announcement, Meta will become UFC's first Official Fan Technology Partner and the Official AI Glasses Partner. The partnership is set to include activations using Meta AI, Meta Glasses, Meta Quest, Facebook, Instagram, WhatsApp and Threads.

The agreement will embed Meta into UFC's global events, including all Pay-Per-View and Fight Night broadcasts, with brand placement in the Octagon and other broadcast elements, UFC said. In addition, Meta will collaborate on in-arena activations and immersive fan experiences.

Meta's text-based platform Threads will also be designated as UFC's Official Social Media Partner. The company said Threads will feature exclusive original content tied to major UFC moments and serve as a primary hub for online fan conversations.

UFC President and Chief Executive Officer Dana White said the organization has begun working with Meta on a new fighter rankings system, which will be unveiled soon. He said Meta's technology will elevate the fan experience and called the collaboration a "game changer" for the sport.

Meta founder and Chief Executive Officer Mark Zuckerberg said he looks forward to bringing fans closer to UFC in new ways.

The financial terms of the deal were not disclosed. Additional elements of the partnership are expected to be released over time as the companies collaborate on further developments.

data.title: Uniti Shareholders Approve Merger With Windstream
data.description:
Uniti said Wednesday its shareholders approved the planned merger with an affiliate of Windstream Holdings II at a special meeting.

More than 90% of shares present and represented at the meeting voted in favor of the merger, the company said.

The deal is expected to close in the second half of 2025, pending regulatory approvals.

data.title: Planet Based Foods Announces Strategic Reorganization
data.description:
Vancouver, British Columbia--(Newsfile Corp. - April 2, 2025) - Planet Based Foods Global Inc. (OTC Pink: PBFFF) (FSE: AZ00) (the "Company"), a company that offers a wide range of hemp-based food alternatives, is pleased to announce it has entered into a Share Purchase Agreement (the "Share Purchase Agreement") with its wholly-owned subsidiary, Planet Based Foods Inc. ("Subco"), a private California-based company, and Braelyn Davis (CEO and director), Robert Davis (CIO) and Ted Cash (CFO and director) (together, the "Purchasers"), pursuant to which the Company will transfer all of the issued and outstanding shares of Subco to the Purchasers in exchange for the surrender by the Purchasers of all of the securities of the Company held by them (the "Transaction"). As a result, an aggregate of 800,000 multiple voting shares and 600,000 subordinate voting shares in the capital of the Company will be returned to treasury and cancelled.

In connection with the closing of the Share Purchase Agreement, the Company and Subco have agreed to enter into an intellectual property assignment agreement, pursuant to which Subco will transfer to the Company all of its intellectual property, including certain proprietary recipes and trademarks (the "Intellectual Property") in exchange for the forgiveness of approximately $5.5 million in intercompany debt. The Company will also grant Subco an exclusive license for the use of the Intellectual Property within the United States, in exchange for a royalty on the revenues derived from Subco's use of the same.

The Company intends to obtain securityholder approval of the Transaction by way of consent resolution in accordance with subsection 4.6(1)(b) of the Canadian Securities Exchange's Policy 4 - Corporate Governance Securityholder Approvals and Miscellaneous Provisions. Completion of the Transaction is subject to, among other things, the satisfaction or waiver of certain closing conditions set forth in the agreement. On closing of the Transaction, it is expected that Messrs. Braelyn Davis, James Harris and Gregory Maselli will resign from the board of directors of the Company, and Mr. Braelyn Davis and Robert Davis will step down from their positions as Chief Executive Officer and Chief Innovation Officer, respectively.

The Transaction is expected to mark a significant step forward in the Company's strategic development. Subject to the satisfaction of all conditions precedent, the Transaction is expected to close in April 2025. A copy of the Share Purchase Agreement will be available under the Company's profile on SEDAR+ at www.sedarplus.ca.

MI 61-101 Considerations

The Transaction constitutes a "related party transaction" for purposes of Multilateral Instrument 61-101 - Protection of Minority Security Holders in Special Transactions ("MI 61-101"), as Subco and the Purchasers are each a "related party", for purposes of MI 61-101. In accordance with MI 61-101, absent an exemption, MI 61-101 would require the Company to obtain a formal and "majority of the minority" shareholder approval ("Disinterested Shareholder Approval") to proceed with the Transaction. The Company is is relying on the exemption in Section 5.5(b) of MI 61-101, in that its shares are not listed on the specified markets and accordingly it is exempt from the "valuation" requirement under MI 61-101. Furthermore, the Company is relying on the exemption in Section 5.7(1)(c) of MI 61-101, in that the Transaction is supported by an Arm's Length Control Person (as the term is defined in MI 61-101), and accordingly is exempt from Disinterested Shareholder Approval.

About the Company

The Company is a producer of sustainable plant-based food alternatives. The Company was founded in 2018 in San Diego, California, with a mission to build a better food system by providing hemp-formulated superfood products to people today that support our planet tomorrow. All of the Company's products are 100% vegan. The Company creates clean, non-GMO, nutrient-dense food built for the future: the first line of plant-based foods with sustainable hemp as the number-one ingredient. Founded by former nutrition coach and cannabis marketer, Braelyn Davis, food scientist and plant-based pioneer, Robert Davis, and restaurant industry veteran, Ted Cash, the Company aims to establish hemp as a nutrient-dense protein source to feed the world sustainably for generations to come. The Company's suite of products includes its new consumer line of nutritious frozen foods, plant-based food ingredients and handheld comfort foods for food service and restaurants.

Additional information about the Company is available on SEDAR+ (www.sedarplus.ca). To subscribe to automated email alerts for future news and public filings, visit the Company's website at www.planetbasedfoods.com.

On Behalf of the Board

Braelyn Davis

CEO and Co-Founder

Contact:

info@planetbasedfoods.com

Forward-Looking Statements

Certain statements included in this news release constitute "Forward-Looking Statements" within the meaning of the United States Private Securities Litigation Reform Act of 1995 and "forward-looking information" under applicable Canadian securities laws (collectively, "forward-looking statements"). Such statements include those identified by the expressions "anticipate", "assume" "believe", "plan", "estimate", "expect", "intend", "may", "should" and similar expressions. The forward-looking statements are not historical facts but reflect current expectations regarding future results or events. These forward-looking statements or information relate to, among other things, shareholder approval of the Transaction and the successful completion of the Transaction.

These statements reflect the Company's current views with respect to future events and are necessarily based upon a number of other assumptions and estimates that, while considered reasonable by management, are inherently subject to significant business, economic, competitive, political and social uncertainties and contingencies. Many factors, both known and unknown, could cause actual results, performance or achievements to be materially different from the results, performance or achievements that are or may be expressed or implied by such forward-looking statements or forward-looking information and the Company has made assumptions and estimates based on or related to many of these factors. Such factors include, without limitation, development of the industry in which the Company operates; risks associated with the conduct of the Company's business activities; risks relating to reliance on the Company's management team and outside contractors; currency fluctuations; risks regarding the failure to generate sufficient cash flow from operations; laws and regulations governing the industry in which the Company operates; operating or technical difficulties; employee relations, labour unrest or unavailability; stock market volatility; conflicts of interest among certain directors and officers; lack of liquidity for shareholders of the Company; litigation risk; and other risk factors disclosed in the Company's public disclosure documents available on the Company's profile at www.sedarplus.ca.

Readers are cautioned against attributing undue certainty to forward-looking statements or forward-looking information. Although the Company has attempted to identify important factors that could cause actual results to differ materially, there may be other factors that cause results not to be anticipated, estimated or intended. The Company does not intend and does not assume any obligation to update these forward-looking statements or forward-looking information to reflect changes in assumptions or changes in circumstances or any other events affecting such statements or information other than as required by applicable law.

The Canadian Securities Exchange does not accept responsibility for the adequacy or accuracy of this news release.

To view the source version of this press release, please visit https://www.newsfilecorp.com/release/247153

data.title: How investors can navigate stagflation worries sparked by White House policies
data.description:
By Christine Idzelis

Investors are anxious about higher tariffs under Trump 2.0, including uncertain economic consequences after 'liberation day'

White House policies have stirred up market worries that investors will face potentially stagflationary shifts in the economy, stemming in part from larger tariffs.

The current White House policy mix is "unambiguously going to hurt U.S. growth in the short term," or over the next six to 12 months, said Bob Elliott, co-founder, chief executive and chief investment officer at Unlimited Funds, in a phone interview. The policies - including tariffs, immigration restrictions and cuts to spending and the federal workforce - point to a "negative picture" for economic growth in the coming months, even if the longer-term outcome is less clear, he said.

For investors worried about the risk of higher inflation and slower growth leading to a stagflationary situation, gold (GC00) typically outperforms relative to other asset classes in that environment, according to Elliott. The yellow metal's prices have risen to all-time highs in 2025 amid volatile markets, yet "gold ownership remains very modest" considering most financial advisers in the U.S. have "essentially zero allocation" to it in their clients' portfolios, he said.

Market volatility has climbed amid fears over global trade wars following recent tariff developments under President Donald Trump this year. Trump has referred to Wednesday's highly anticipated announcement concerning a new set of tariffs as "liberation day," with investors on edge over their potential size, scope and resulting repercussions for economic activity in the U.S.

Many investors worry that large tariffs risk damaging economic growth and increasing inflation. And memories of losses for both stocks and bonds in 2022 - when a surge in inflation during the pandemic prompted the Federal Reserve to aggressively hike interest rates - remain top of mind as investors consider ways to protect their portfolios from market volatility.

So far this year, gold prices have soared while U.S. stocks are down and bonds are up.

Check out: This gold ETF jumps to record after its longest winning streak since 2020 amid tariff concerns

The "negative growth aspects" of the White House's pursued policies have recently overwhelmed their inflationary risks, "creating a rally in bonds," said Elliott.

The iShares Core U.S. Aggregate Bond ETF AGG, an exchange-traded fund that provides broad exposure to the U.S. investment-grade bond market, has posted a total return 2.9% this year as of Wednesday afternoon, according to FactSet data. By contrast, the S&P 500 SPX has dropped around 4% in 2025.

Bond diversification

Despite recent worries over sticky inflation, bonds are providing diversification benefits in portfolios consisting of a traditional mix of equities and fixed-income assets so far this year.

That's partly because investors appear more concerned about slowing U.S. growth than a potential pickup in inflation - but bonds also are benefiting from higher starting yields as the Fed holds its benchmark interest-rate steady so far in 2025, said Greg Hall, Pimco's head of U.S. global wealth management, in an interview.

At this point, the Fed seems more likely to cut its policy rate this year than to hike it, said Hall.

That means fixed income may offer some "insurance in your portfolio," he said, explaining that if the Fed does cut rates this year, bonds should see some price appreciation as a result.

Recession fears recently spurred investors to seek safety in the U.S. bond market, sending Treasury yields lower, according to emailed comments on Tuesday from Louis Navellier, chief investment officer of Navellier & Associates. Bond yields and prices move in opposite directions.

See: Bond rates are falling in 'a flight to quality' ahead of new Trump tariffs expected April 2

Meanwhile, investors are getting higher yield from fixed-income assets today than during the period of low interest rates seen before the pandemic-era spike in inflation.The higher bond yields are "attractive" from an income perspective while also providing some cushion should fixed-income assets sell off a bit on inflation worries, according to Hall.

While the Fed's summary economic projections in March pointed to potentially 50 basis points of cuts to the central bank's policy rate this year, elevated inflation against the backdrop of slowing growth puts the Fed in the challenging position. Inflation stuck above its 2% target risks making it more difficult for the Fed to cut interest rates to support its full-employment goal should unemployment rise in an economic slowdown.

When surging inflation wreaked havoc in markets in 2022, shorter-duration fixed-income assets fared better than longer-duration bonds. For example, while the iShares Core U.S. Aggregate Bond ETF lost a total of 13% that year, the Pimco Enhanced Short Maturity Active ETF MINT saw a total loss of 1%, while the SPDR Bloomberg 1-3 Month T-Bill ETF BIL returned a total of 1.4%, according to FactSet data.

Now it's more likely that "you get an environment where growth is weak" and the Fed is "slow to react because the inflationary impulse of the tariff policies keeps inflation a little too high," said Elliott. That means the Fed would be "cautious to cut aggressively until they see a meaningful weakening of growth."

Venturing beyond traditional assets?

Investors also increasingly have access to alternative strategies in the exchange-traded-fund industry, including those with a hedge-fund style designed to help portfolios navigate tumultuous times. Such strategies tend to be more expensive than traditional stock and bond ETFs.

See: This hedge-fund-style ETF is aimed at investors looking to diversify in volatile markets. Here's what to look for.

Macro hedge funds, which have flexibility to bet across asset classes globally, currently hold the "the most significant long gold positions" than they have in many decades, according to Elliott.

He said that Unlimited is preparing to launch an ETF that tracks the positions of the largest global macro managers while targeting twice the returns of a related index. Elliott said he expects that the Unlimited HFGM Global Macro ETF, which trades futures contracts to take long or short positions across stocks, bonds, currencies and commodities, will launch in mid-April.

One advantage of global macro strategies, long used in the hedge-fund world, is that they're designed to have a low correlation to stocks, said Elliott.

The U.S. stock market was trading up on Wednesday afternoon, with the Dow Jones Industrial Average DJIA rising 0.7%, the S&P 500 climbing 0.8% and the technology-heavy Nasdaq Composite COMP advancing 1%, according to FactSet data.

The SPDR Gold Shares GLD, an exchange-traded fund that buys gold, was rising modestly Wednesday afternoon, bringing its year-to-date gain to around 19%.

In the bond market, the yield on the 10-year Treasury note BX:TMUBMUSD10Y was rising about two basis points on Wednesday afternoon to around 4.19%. For the year, though, the 10-year Treasury yield has declined about 42 basis points through Tuesday based on 3 p.m. Eastern levels, according to Dow Jones Market Data.

Investors were bracing for Trump to announce his "liberation day" tariffs after the stock market's close on Wednesday.

-Christine Idzelis

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Tech, Media & Telecom Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Technology, Media and Telecom. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

1559 ET - Canada should brace for an indefinite pause in business investment, says former Bank of Canada Gov. Stephen Poloz, citing uncertainty due to President Trump's tariff policy. Trump is set to unveil shortly his broad tariff plan, and he has repeatedly pledged 25% tariffs on all nonenergy Canadian imports. Any investment from Canadian firms in the coming months is likely to be "defensive," Poloz tells Canada's CTV News. "It may be investment dollars headed to [the US] to help buttress or build a natural hedge for the company." Poloz says the timing is unfortunate as the global economy enters a fourth industrial revolution, fueled by investment in artificial intelligence. "We run the risk of missing out on that whole wave of investment, which would be very productivity enhancing," Poloz says. (paul.vieira@wsj.com; @paulvieira)

1222 ET - Apple is benefiting from higher App store revenue in its fiscal 2Q, according to data from SensorTower, which could boost its service topline performance, BofA Securities analysts say in a research note. The company, which has faced slumping iPhone sales in recent months, saw App store revenue up 15% to $8.6 billion in 2Q, with total downloads rising 5% to $8.9 billion. This data show the company is in a position of stable cash flows, earnings resiliency, and potential beneficiary of AI use on edge devices, the analysts say. Furthermore, Apple's recent availability of the Apple TV app on Android mobile devices could help it increase subscription revenue. BofA expect services revenue growth of 12% and 13% in the 2Q and FY25, respectively, but the estimates could prove conservative if App store strength persists, the analysts add. (sabela.ojea@wsj.com; @sabelaojeaguix)

1112 ET - The U.S. median home price is $412,000, requiring 10 workdays monthly to afford the mortgage, says Realtor.com, but for residents in some states, the number of workdays required can go up to 17 days. Hawaii's $796,947 median demands 17 days for a $5,222 payment, while California--$4,773--and Montana--$613,275--need 15 days each, driven by demand and rising costs in places like Bozeman. Conversely, the Midwest and Southeast offer relief. West Virginia--$247,000--and Ohio--$259,450--require just seven and six days, respectively. Other affordable states--Kansas, Missouri, Indiana, Illinois, and Michigan--also average a week's work for mortgage payments, highlighting stark regional disparities in housing affordability. (chris.wack@wsj.com)

0859 ET - BlackBerry is investing more into expanding beyond automotive for its embedded systems software offering. On an analyst call, executives say that there is "real interest pipeline and opportunities coming our way," as the company looks to bring its software to new sectors such as in medical and robotics. They say that the software is often a natural fit, with little modification needed to adapt to new industries. Currently, the company is investing in go-to-market, marketing and sales push efforts, but that it isn't leaving its automotive customers in the lurch. "At the same time, we're going deeper with our automotive customers," executives say. (adriano.marchese@wsj.com)

0857 ET - BlackBerry says its customers are feeling their way through the tariff situation, but there isn't panic yet. Executives on an analyst call say that the OEMs it works with are "taking direction from what's happening in the market place," but that no one has called for a downtick in production. They say that the uncertainty has OEMs on alert, but so far "nobody has raised the flag of a material downturn, everyone trying to read the tea leaves of where this will go in the future and what the impact will be." (adriano.marchese@wsj.com)

0848 ET - BlackBerry says that it can partially mitigate the effects of US tariffs, but that its customers may not be as lucky. On an analyst call, executives say that automotive tariffs won't affect the business directly, but that it will affect its customers in the industry, along the supply chain. BlackBerry says that it can mitigate to some extent to US-specific tariff effects because 50% of its revenue from the embedded-systems-software unit QNX comes from outside of the US. Revenue from the unit is expected at $250 million to $270 million. (adriano.marchese@wsj.com)

0822 ET - There are a host of bidders for TikTok as a deadline to find a non-Chinese buyer looms, but any deal will likely be structured and centered around Oracle and its cofounder Larry Ellison, Wedbush analysts say in a research note. Oracle is already TikTok's primary cloud provider and it has gone down an M&A path with TikTok before during Trump's first administration, the analysts say. They believe the platform's algorithm will be kept under ByteDance, but Oracle will be used to safeguard U.S. consumer data on TikTok. ByteDance and overall Chinese ownership will likely be a minority stake, but could be higher than 20%, the analysts say. Leading TikTok investors from the private-equity world would clearly be involved too, they say. (dean.seal@wsj.com)

0734 ET - Domino's Pizza will start marketing its pies on DoorDash as it seeks to reach new customers, particularly suburban and rural ones who have access to the app. U.S. customers will be able to order Domino's across DoorDash markets starting next month, with Domino's delivering the pies. Domino's first started marketing its pizza on Uber Eats in 2023. (heather.haddon@wsj.com; @heatherhaddon)

0440 ET - Raspberry Pi's inventory correction has now largely ended and orders are starting to gradually improve as a result, Jefferies analysts Janardan Menon and Om Bakhda write. High inventory dragged on the low-cost computer maker's results for the majority of last year, they write. The company is also yet to be affected by U.S. tariffs and with most competitors' manufacturing based in Asia, Raspberry Pi's Welsh factory may even provide a small advantage as long as demand in the U.S. remains strong, they write. Shares trade up 8.8% at 512 pence.(adam.whittaker@wsj.com)

0324 ET - The White House could announce the outline of a deal as the deadline for TikTok to find a non-Chinese buyer approaches this week, Wedbush analysts say in a research note. Wedbush views TikTok "as one of the biggest and first chips on the poker table around US/China relations." With tariff announcements slated for Wednesday, the TikTok situation must be carefully handled by the Trump administration to keep a friendly U.S.-China tone heading into a potential meeting likely in June, they say. The analysts think Oracle may be the potential buyer as it is already TikTok's primary cloud provider. TikTok's algorithm will likely be kept under ByteDance but Oracle will be a linchpin to safeguarding U.S. consumer data on TikTok, Wedbush adds. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

0233 ET - Vodafone Idea stands to benefit from India's Ministry of Communications' decision to convert INR369.5 billion of outstanding spectrum dues into equity, Nomura's Hemang Khanna says in a research report, as the brokerage maintains the stock's buy rating. The decision looks positive as this eases worries over the Indian telecom company's managing repayments of spectrum dues in FY 2026 and improves the outlook for debt raising, the analyst says. With the ministry's action, the company's spectrum dues over FY 2026-2028 have fallen to roughly INR200 billion from INR610 billion previously, the analyst notes. However, Nomura trims its FY 2025-2027 Ebitda forecasts for Vodafone Idea by 4% to reflect modestly lower subscribers' assumptions, and lowers its target price to INR10.00 from INR12.00. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

2319 ET - KCE Electronics is likely to face an earnings hit from the 25% auto tariffs from the U.S. that are expected to take effect this Wednesday, Maybank Securities (Thailand)'s Yugi Takeshima says in a research report. Given that at least 50% of the Thai company's revenue comes from the West's automotive sector, the brokerage estimate that if the tariffs are permanent, they would probably have lasting repercussions. Citing a likely slump in demand from the supply chain shock in the global automotive sector from the tariffs, the brokerage cuts its 2025 core earnings forecast for the printed-circuit-board supplier to the automotive industry by 24%. It downgrades the stock's rating to sell from buy and lowers the target price to THB13.90 from THB27.00. Shares last closed at THB16.00. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: Health Care Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering the Health Care sector. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

1317 ET - Hims & Hers is hoping to take advantage of an FDA rule to be able to keep selling compounded versions of weight-loss drugs, even after the GLP-1 shortage has ended, Needham analyst Ryan MacDonald tells WSJ. The personalization exemption rule allows companies to sell compounds if they are personalizing the treatment for a patient; for example, if the patient needs to take an oral form instead of an injection. Hims hopes it can keep offering personalized compounds, as compounded drugs make up a significant portion of its weight-loss revenue, MacDonald says. But if the FDA finds this illegal, the company may be challenged to meet revenue goals, MacDonald says. (katherine.hamilton@wsj.com)

1314 ET - Hims & Hers' launch of generic liraglutide is likely to contribute more to the telehealth platform's growth than offering Eli Lilly's Zepbound, Leerink Partners analyst Michael Cherny tells WSJ. The liraglutide launch comes a month earlier than expected and could help boost quarterly revenue, as the company is looking for alternatives to its compounded semaglutide drug, which the FDA is cracking down on now that shortages of GLP-1s are over. Liraglutide falls more in line with Hims business model of offering affordable treatments, Cherney says, as it would cost around $299 a month vs. $1,899 a month for Zepbound.(katherine.hamilton@wsj.com)

0919 ET - U.K. pharmaceutical stocks could perform better than their European counterparts in the short and medium term if the U.K. gets a chance to bargain for lighter tariffs, XTB's Kathleen Brooks says in a note. The U.K. is better positioned to negotiate favorable terms with the U.S. as it has a small trade deficit with the U.S., especially compared to Germany, she says. If it looks likely that the U.K. could negotiate a lower tariff rate with the U.S. compared to the EU, then pharma giant GSK could be one notable candidate that could outperform, she says. Other U.K. pharmaceutical stocks like Smith and Nephew, and AstraZeneca are likely to benefit, she says. (miriam.mukuru@wsj.com)

0725 ET - Novo Nordisk is a "screaming buy," AlphaValue analyst Abhishek Raval writes. The company has a lead in the obesity market, a solid pipeline, aggressive intent toward licensing deals, and a valuation lower than both Novartis and AstraZeneca, he says. Underwhelming late-stage results from obesity candidate CagriSema, Trump tariff uncertainty, and concerns around a guidance downgrade due to competitive pressures have all weighed on Novo's share price. Nonetheless, AlphaValue has already baked some caution into 2025 estimates and still expects Novo's bottom line to register a 16% compound annual growth rate, which would be the highest among European big pharma over 2024-2027. AlphaValue lowers its target price on the stock to 943 Danish kroner from 981 kroner and retains its buy rating. Shares fall 2.4% to 467.75 kroner. (dominic.chopping@wsj.com)

0637 ET - Major pharmaceutical stocks GSK and AstraZeneca, alongside aerospace and defense stocks Rolls-Royce and Melrose, are among the biggest FTSE 100 fallers ahead of Trump's tariff announcement later Wednesday. These stocks would be "in the firing line if Trump doesn't give the U.K. special treatment," says AJ Bell's Russ Mould in a note. Many investors hope the U.K. could be spared the worst of the tariffs. However, if U.K. Prime Minister Keir Starmer fails to secure a better deal, companies within pharma, food and drink, aviation, chemical and automotive sectors would be vulnerable, he says. Rolls-Royce is the biggest FTSE 100 faller, down 3.4%; GSK falls 3.2%; Melrose loses 2.6% and AstraZeneca is down 2.3%. The FTSE 100 falls 0.7%. (jessica.fleetham@wsj.com)

data.title: H&R Block Names Richard Johnson as New Chairman
data.description:
By Sabela Ojea

H&R Block named Richard Johnson as its new chairman of the board, replacing longtime Chairman Robert Gerard, who served in the role since 2011.

The tax preparation company on Wednesday said the appointment of Johnson comes as the firm transitions to its next five-year strategy for growth and transformation.

"We are well-positioned for the future and the next chapter of our growth strategy as we look to fiscal year 2026 and beyond," Chief Executive Jeff Jones said.

Johnson joined the H&R Block board of directors in 2015 and is a member of the audit and compensation committees.

Write to Sabela Ojea at sabela.ojea@wsj.com; @sabelaojeaguix

data.title: Financial Services Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Financial Services. Exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

1112 ET - The U.S. median home price is $412,000, requiring 10 workdays monthly to afford the mortgage, says Realtor.com, but for residents in some states, the number of workdays required can go up to 17 days. Hawaii's $796,947 median demands 17 days for a $5,222 payment, while California--$4,773--and Montana--$613,275--need 15 days each, driven by demand and rising costs in places like Bozeman. Conversely, the Midwest and Southeast offer relief. West Virginia--$247,000--and Ohio--$259,450--require just seven and six days, respectively. Other affordable states--Kansas, Missouri, Indiana, Illinois, and Michigan--also average a week's work for mortgage payments, highlighting stark regional disparities in housing affordability. (chris.wack@wsj.com)

1038 ET - The Energy Information Administration says production of uranium concentrate in the U.S. in 2024 was the highest since 2018, due in part to climbing uranium prices. The EIA says that two facilities, one in Texas and one in Wyoming, were responsible for the uptick, along with a Utah facility that resumed operation last year. Commodity traders have had a growing interest ways to trade uranium, prompting a number of new ETFs and attempts at offerings in the crypto market. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

0944 ET - Spain's Santander hasn't specified what its strategy in Canada could be but its other international markets provide insights on how it might proceed, Morningstar DBRS says in a note. Canada's regulator allowed Santander Consumer Bank to start and carry on business in early March. The lender already has car-financing operations in the country. "The bank's strategy is likely to focus on specific niches, leverage Santander's global expertise in consumer/retail financing, and incorporate significant use of digital technologies, not unlike its current approach in the U.S. and Mexico," analyst Sailie Ashtekar writes. However, any pursuit of a stronger foothold in Canada's banking scene will need long-term commitments and realistic expectations given it is a competitive market, she says. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

0902 ET - Rathbones is trading at a material discount to its peers despite its stronger near-term growth and capital returns at sector-leading levels, RBC Capital Markets says. The integration of Investec Wealth is nearly done and should translate into more meaningful earnings benefits from this year onwards, though this isn't currently reflected in its valuation, analyst Ben Bathurst and associate Jude Neanor write in a research note. "Asset gathering has been depressed through the integration process but we expect a return to positive net flows from 2H FY25 onwards, which we would expect to be supportive to sentiment," they note. RBC raises its rating on the stock to outperform from sector perform and its target price to 2,000 pence from 1,850 pence. Shares trade at 1,550 pence and are down 6.6% year to date. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

0544 ET - Tryg is not such a safe haven after news that the Danish Competition Council is consulting on a possible investigation into Danish retail property and casualty insurers, Citi analysts James A Shuck and Emanuele Musio write. "Investors gravitate to Nordic insurers as safe-haven plays, and uncertainty undermines this." Initial concerns have some validity, in the bank's view, and there is a greater risk that this could permeate through other Nordic territories beyond Denmark. Citi says this is the first time it's ever seen Nordic regulators look into perceived excessive margins in insurance. "We are now wary of recommending any Nordic insurer." It downgrades Tryg to neutral from buy and lowers its target price to 152 Danish kroner from 180.50 kroner. Shares fall 3.8% to 153.70 kroner. (dominic.chopping@wsj.com)

0423 ET - Credit Agricole getting European Central Bank authorization to increase its stake in Banco BPM was expected and bolsters the French bank's negotiating position, Equita Sim analyst Andrea Lisi says. The move allows Credit Agricole to increase its stake to up to 19.9% of the Italian bank. The stake increase reinforces Credit Agricole's negotiating position with regard to Italian peer UniCredit, the analyst says in a research note. UniCredit, which is separately offering to buy BPM shares, will reasonably need to reach an agreement with Credit Agricole to increase the likelihood of a successful bid, the analyst says. Credit Agricole shares trade 1% lower at 16.85 euros and BPM shares are down 1.7% at 9.38 euros. UniCredit shares, meanwhile, trade down 1.65% at 51.80 euros. (pierre.bertrand@wsj.com)

2212 ET - Digital Telecommunications Infrastructure Fund, also known as DIF, looks attractive, Thanachart Securities' Nuttapop Prasitsuksant says in a research report. The year-to-date correction of DIF's unit price has lifted the brokerage's dividend yield estimate to a very attractive level of 11% for 2025. Also, the unit price's decline is in contrast to DIF's stable cash flows, which are supported by steady rental revenue from its long-term contracts, the analyst says. Moreover, the brokerage expects further Bank of Thailand rate cuts to act as a unit price catalyst. The brokerage reaffirms the unit's buy rating but trims the target to THB10.00 from THB10.50 to reflect valuation base year of 2025. Units last closed at THB7.80. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

2125 ET - Malaysia's banking sector may remain resilient, with loan growth likely to accelerate later in 2025 as larger approved investments materialize, Hong Leong IB analyst Chan Jit Hoong says in a note. Net interest margins should hold steady in 1Q as banks adopt a disciplined lending strategy and ease deposit competition, he reckons. Asset quality may remain supported by strong economic conditions and ample loan loss provisions, he says. The sector, offering a 5% dividend yield, may remain attractively valued and serve as a hedge against Trump 2.0's risks, he adds. Hong Leong maintains an overweight rating on the sector, with buy calls on Affin Bank, AMMB Holdings, Bank Islam Malaysia, CIMB Group, Malayan Banking, Public Bank, and RHB Bank. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

1941 ET - The revenue benefits that Westpac derives from its multiyear tech overhaul will partly depend on the response of its Australian banking peers, Morgan Stanley analysts warn. They write in a note that the project has significant execution risk and see a chance that it will cost more and save less than the lender expects. They point out that the initiative is currently expected to cost about A$3 billion, take five years to implement, involve 60 initiatives, cover three divisions, and be implemented by a new management team. Other major banks will respond in their efforts to maintain or grow their share of core markets, they say. MS has an underweight recommendation on Westpac. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

data.title: Energy & Utilities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Energy and Utilities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

1508 ET - Oil futures rise as the market shrugs off a 6.2 million barrel build in U.S. crude stocks while impending U.S. tariffs on Canadian and Mexican oil, and sanctions against Venezuela, Russia and Iran keep supply concerns alive. "Every crude oil input was a bearish number in this week's report," Mizuho's Robert Yawger says in a note, as U.S. crude imports rose, exports fell, and refineries processed less crude than the week before. "All the moving pieces rarely line up on the same side of the ledger." WTI settles up 0.7% at $71.71 a barrel, and Brent rises 0.6% to $74.95 a barrel.(anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

0943 ET - Bank of America says it expects most ag products to have a weaker showing in the upcoming month, with the exception of soybean meal, which the firm projects as having a "positive return" along with other commodities like Brent crude, gasoline, sugar, and copper. As other analysts point out, soybean meal futures are in contango--meaning that prices for the product in the future are more expensive than they are on the present-day spot market. This morning, the USDA announced a new flash sale of soybean meal exports to the Philippines, with 135,000 metric tons of soy cake and meal sold for delivery in the 2024/25 marketing year. Soybean meal futures are down 0.4% while underlying soybeans are down 0.3% in early trading on the CBOT. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

0920 ET - Crude futures are lower with the market awaiting U.S. inventory data and major tariff announcements from the White House. "Price trends may be upended after the market digests tariffs and their impact on the crude oil market today," Alex Hodes of StoneX says in a note. Analysts note a large and unexpected 6 million barrel increase in U.S. crude stocks reported yesterday by the API that's also weighing on prices. Closely watched EIA data are due at 10:30 a.m. ET, with analysts in a Wall Street Journal survey forecasting a 700,000 barrel draw. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

0620 ET - Nel's announcement that Samsung E&A took a 10% stake and signed a collaboration agreement is an encouraging step in providing the hydrogen company further headroom on its balance sheet, Citi analyst Vivek Midha writes. "The addition of a new anchor shareholder also provides further comfort to investors." However, the top-line outlook remains sluggish, with weak order intake and risk of delays and cancellations within the backlog, Citi says. Nel trades at a clear premium to peers as investors price in a clear recovery in volumes, the analyst writes. The bank resumes coverage of Nel's shares with a neutral rating and 2.50 Norwegian kroner target price. Shares fall 1.8% to 2.38 kroner. (dominic.chopping@wsj.com)

0552 ET - Shell's preference for share buybacks over dividends could become an issue for yield-oriented investors, HSBC analysts write. U.S. investors are typically more comfortable with buybacks than European investors, the analysts add. The analysts say that they are sympathetic to Shell's view that buying back shares is a good way generate per-share growth in a mature industry. However, the move will cause dividend yields to fall, they write. Shares trade flat at 2,796 pence. (adam.whittaker@wsj.com)

0527 ET - Shell's improved capital discipline will deliver higher long-term shareholder distributions, but there is little near-term upside, HSBC analysts write. At last month's capital market day, Shell kept its dividend guidance of 4% growth a year. However, it is expected to increase dividend growth to around 10% a year from the end of 2028 as free cash flow grows, the analysts write. The analysts expect Shell to buy back 7% of its share count in 2025, before increasing that ratio to buy 12% by 2030. The analysts expect Shell to repurchase $16 billion of shares in 2029 and $17 billion in 2030. As Shell continues to favor buybacks over dividends, its dividend yield will fall, the analysts write. Shares trade flat at 2,796 pence. (adam.whittaker@wsj.com)

0432 ET - SSE updated adjusted EPS guidance of a 155 pence to 160 pence range might be slightly disappointing for the market, with Bloomberg consensus of 159 pence at the top end of it, J.P. Morgan Cazenove analysts say in a note. The energy group previously guided for a 154 to 163 pence range. Although the company is well-placed to benefit from U.K.'s policy and regulatory headwinds, there is uncertainty stemming from the potential reforms of the wholesale power market, despite these unlikely having any effect before 2030, the analysts say. "We remain positive on the SSE equity story... as we see concerns around the balance sheet and zonal pricing [a mechanism that regulates electricity prices] as overdone," JPM Cazenove says. Shares are up 0.7% at 1,592 pence.(anthony.orunagoriainoff@dowjones.com)

2319 ET - KCE Electronics is likely to face an earnings hit from the 25% auto tariffs from the U.S. that are expected to take effect this Wednesday, Maybank Securities (Thailand)'s Yugi Takeshima says in a research report. Given that at least 50% of the Thai company's revenue comes from the West's automotive sector, the brokerage estimate that if the tariffs are permanent, they would probably have lasting repercussions. Citing a likely slump in demand from the supply chain shock in the global automotive sector from the tariffs, the brokerage cuts its 2025 core earnings forecast for the printed-circuit-board supplier to the automotive industry by 24%. It downgrades the stock's rating to sell from buy and lowers the target price to THB13.90 from THB27.00. Shares last closed at THB16.00. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

2302 ET - Petronas Gas may see a short-term share price decline of 1.8%-2.1% following the gas pipeline fire in Selangor state, but long-term recovery will depend on how effectively it manages the aftermath, MIDF Research says in a note. It estimates total financial impact at 1%-1.5% of Petronas Gas' earnings and an one-off impairment of about MYR18 million-MYR25 million, with minimal operational disruptions as gas flow is diverted. The research house expects additional costs of at least MYR5 million over two-three months for compensating industrial customers directly impacted by the damaged pipeline. MIDF maintains a buy rating on Petronas Gas and keeps target price at MYR18.67, citing the company's prompt response and expected adherence to planned recovery timeline. Shares are 1.8% lower at MYR16.58. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

data.title: Cyclacel Pharmaceuticals Reports Fourth Quarter Financial Results And Provides Business Update
data.description:
Cyclacel Pharmaceuticals Inc :

  • CYCLACEL PHARMACEUTICALS REPORTS FOURTH QUARTER FINANCIAL RESULTS AND PROVIDES BUSINESS UPDATE

data.title: Auto & Transport Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering the Auto and Transport sector. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

1212 ET - Tesla's 1Q production and delivery report was "a disaster on every metric" at a time when the brand already faces growing negativity, Dan Ives and other Wedbush analysts say in a research note. Wall Street was bracing for a bad result but the quarter was even worse than expected, the analysts say. The more political CEO Elon Musk gets with DOGE, the more the brand suffers, as evidenced by the spate of protests at Tesla dealerships and violence seen toward Tesla drivers, the analysts say. They see this as a moment of truth for Musk to navigate the brand crisis and "get onto the other side of this dark chapter for Tesla with much better days ahead," according to the note. (dean.seal@wsj.com)

1153 ET - Tesla's retooling of four factories for its new Model Y was likely a significant factor in the 13% decline in Tesla deliveries during 1Q, Baird analysts Ben Kallo and Davis Sunderland say in a research note. The retooling led to several weeks of downtime, about 4 to 6 weeks depending on the factory and local supply chain, the analysts say. Production is now underway at each factory and ramping smoothly, but the ramp-up will likely spill into 2Q some, they say. The analysts reduce their gross margin estimates for the rest of the year to account for factory underutilization. (dean.seal@wsj.com)

0848 ET - BlackBerry says that it can partially mitigate the effects of US tariffs, but that its customers may not be as lucky. On an analyst call, executives say that automotive tariffs won't affect the business directly, but that it will affect its customers in the industry, along the supply chain. BlackBerry says that it can mitigate to some extent to US-specific tariff effects because 50% of its revenue from the embedded-systems-software unit QNX comes from outside of the US. Revenue from the unit is expected at $250 million to $270 million. (adriano.marchese@wsj.com)

0756 ET - Retailers B&M European Value Retail and Halfords, as well as holiday company Jet2, are all potential takeover targets, AJ Bell investment analyst Dan Coatsworth says, citing falls in their share prices. B&M and Halfords' shares are down 49% and 19%, respectively, over the past year, while Jet2 shares are 14% lower. "Someone looking to own any of these businesses would look at risks and rewards years down the line, not merely what's around the corner," the analyst says. (ian.walker@wsj.com.)

0635 ET - Porsche is preparing itself for U.S. tariffs with pre-shipments and planned adjustments to the pricing strategy, with some pass-through to customers, Deutsche Bank says after it hosted the company's new CFO. Overall, the bank expects volatility around tariff announcements to remain high. "We understood that the first quarter will be a weak start into the year, with a margin being most likely below the full-year corridor." Porsche doesn't expect significant improvements in China in the medium term, so is introducing mitigation actions like cost reductions to protect its margin, Deutsche Bank says. It rates Porsche at buy with a 70 euro target price. Shares fall 0.4% to 45.44 euros. (dominic.chopping@wsj.com)

0455 ET - RBC Global Asset Management expects U.S. President Trump to announce comprehensive tariff increases, in addition to the measures already announced. A wave of retaliatory measures by other countries is likely to follow, says Andrzej Skiba, head of BlueBay U.S. fixed income within the asset management group. "Even if individual steps could be reversed later through concessions, we assume that the majority will remain in place," he says. The asset management company's baseline scenario envisages an average tariff rate of 10%, ​​with some significantly higher burdens for certain countries and sectors. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

2237 ET - Malaysia's auto sales are forecast to decline 7% to 760,000 units in 2025, CIMB Securities analyst Mohd Shanaz Noor Azam says in a note. That's below the Malaysian Automotive Association's forecast of 780,000 units. Potential changes in petrol subsidies and intensifying competition in the premium auto segment could weigh on sales, he says. However, demand in the mass-market segment is likely to remain strong, he says. The analyst expects potential changes in fuel subsidies will accelerate the adoption of electric vehicles, benefiting players like Sime Darby given its expanding EV portfolio. Sime Darby is CIMB's top pick for the sector. The brokerage maintains a neutral rating on Malaysia's auto sector, citing attractive potential dividend yields of 8.5%-8.7% despite subdued growth prospects. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

data.title: Atlassian Insider Sold Shares Worth $1,691,339, According to a Recent SEC Filing
data.description:
Scott Farquhar, 10% Owner, Director, on April 01, 2025, sold 7,948 shares in Atlassian for $1,691,339. Following the Form 4 filing with the SEC, Farquhar has control over a total of 365,608 Class A common shares of the company, with 365,608 controlled indirectly.

data.title: Solaris Energy Infrastructure, Inc. Shareholder Notice: Robbins LLP Reminds Investors of the Class Action Lawsuit Against SEI
data.description:
SAN DIEGO, April 02, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — Robbins LLP reminds stockholders that a class action was filed on behalf of all persons and entities that purchased or otherwise acquired Solaris Energy Infrastructure, Inc. securities between July 9, 2024 and March 17, 2025. Solaris provides equipment used in the completion of oil and natural gas wells in the United States. On July 9, 2024, Solaris announced that it has entered into an agreement to acquire Mobile Energy Rentals LLC (“MER”).

For more information, submit a form, email attorney Aaron Dumas, Jr., or give us a call at (800) 350-6003.

The Allegations: Robbins LLP is Investigating Allegations that Solaris Energy Infrastructure, Inc. (SEI) Misled Investors Regarding the Value Mobile Energy Rentals LLC Would Bring to the Company

According to the complaint, during the class period, defendants failed to disclose: (1) MER had little to no corporate history in the mobile turbine leasing space; (2) MER did not have a diversified earnings stream; (3) MER’s co-owner was a convicted felon associated with multiple allegations of turbine-related fraud; (4) as a result, Solaris overstated the commercial prospects posed by the Acquisition; and (5) Solaris inflated profitability metrics by failing to properly depreciate its turbines. When the truth was revealed, Solaris’ stock price fell $4.15, or 16.9%, to close at $20.46 per share on March 17, 2025.

What Now: You may be eligible to participate in the class action against Solaris Energy Infrastructure, Inc. Shareholders who want to serve as lead plaintiff for the class must file their papers with the court by May 27, 2025. A lead plaintiff is a representative party who acts on behalf of other class members in directing the litigation. You do not have to participate in the case to be eligible for a recovery. If you choose to take no action, you can remain an absent class member. For more information, click here.

All representation is on a contingency fee basis. Shareholders pay no fees or expenses.  

About Robbins LLP: A recognized leader in shareholder rights litigation, the attorneys and staff of Robbins LLP have been dedicated to helping shareholders recover losses, improve corporate governance structures, and hold company executives accountable for their wrongdoing since 2002.

To be notified if a class action against Solaris Energy Infrastructure, Inc. settles or to receive free alerts when corporate executives engage in wrongdoing, sign up for Stock Watch today.

data.title: Europe Must Change To 'Act As A Single Entity,' ECB's Lagarde Says
data.description:
By Paul Hannon

The European Union must change the way it makes decisions in order to respond to an "inverted world" that threatens its economic wellbeing and its security, the head of the European Central Bank said Wednesday.

In a speech in Ireland, Christine Lagarde said the world now faces a period of "closure, fragmentation and uncertainty" as it leaves behind an era in which the U.S. fostered "openness, integration and certainty."

"Everyone benefited from a hegemon, the United States, that was committed to a multilateral, rules-based order," she said. "This allowed trade and investment to flourish."

However, the rise of protectionism that includes higher U.S. tariffs threatens Europe's export-reliant economy, while it can no longer "fully count" on the U.S. as military ally.

In that new, more hostile environment, Lagarde said it is essential for Europe to remain united.

"At a time when major economies are adopting cohesive strategic agendas - using tariffs, for example, to extract concessions on other strategic goals - Europe cannot afford to be disunited," she said.

At present, individual members of the EU have a veto on decisions across a range of policies that are traditionally regarded as crucial to national interests, including foreign policy.

Lagarde said it is time to expand the use of qualified majority voting, under which a single country can't stand in the way of a course of action favored by the other members.

"Given the geopolitical shift at hand, I am convinced that national and European interests have never been so aligned," she said. "In this inverted world, more qualified majority voting would therefore be inherently more democratic."

Write to Paul Hannon at paul.hannon@wsj.com

data.title: SOUN Class Action Alert: Shareholder Rights Law Firm Robbins LLP Reminds Investors of the Lead Plaintiff Deadline in the SoundHound AI, Inc. Class Action
data.description:
SAN DIEGO, April 02, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — Robbins LLP reminds stockholders that a class action was filed on behalf of all persons and entities that purchased or otherwise acquired SoundHound AI, Inc. securities between May 10, 2024 and March 3, 2025. SoundHound provides an independent voice artificial intelligence (“AI”) platform that purportedly enables businesses across industries to deliver high-quality conversational experiences to their customers.

For more information, submit a form, email attorney Aaron Dumas, Jr., or give us a call at (800) 350-6003.

The Allegations: Robbins LLP is Investigating Allegations that SoundHound AI, Inc. (SOUN) Failed to Disclose it Lacked Effective Controls Relating to Corporate Acquisitions

According to the complaint, during the class period, defendants failed to disclose that: (i) the material weaknesses in SoundHound’s internal controls over financial reporting impaired the Company’s ability to effectively account for corporate acquisitions; (ii) in addition, the Company overstated the extent to which it had remediated, and/or its ability to remediate, the material weaknesses in its internal controls over financial reporting; (iii) as a result of the foregoing material weaknesses, SoundHound’s reported goodwill following the Amelia Acquisition was inflated and would need to be corrected; (iv) further, SoundHound would likely require extra time and expense to effectively account for the SYNQ3 and Amelia Acquisitions; and (v) the foregoing increased the risk that the Company would be unable to timely file certain financial reports with the SEC.

On March 4, 2025, SoundHound disclosed in a filing with the SEC that it would be unable to timely file its Annual Report for 2024. SoundHound stated that “[d]ue to the complexity of accounting for [the SYNQ3 and Amelia Acquisitions], the Company require[d] additional time to prepare financial statements and accompanying notes” and that it “ha[d] identified material weaknesses in its internal control over financial reporting.” On this news, SoundHound’s stock price fell $0.61 per share, or 5.86%, to close at $9.72 per share on March 4, 2025.

What Now: You may be eligible to participate in the class action against SoundHound AI, Inc. Shareholders who want to serve as lead plaintiff for the class should contact Robbins LLP. A lead plaintiff is a representative party who acts on behalf of other class members in directing the litigation. You do not have to participate in the case to be eligible for a recovery. If you choose to take no action, you can remain an absent class member. For more information, click here.

All representation is on a contingency fee basis. Shareholders pay no fees or expenses.

About Robbins LLP: A recognized leader in shareholder rights litigation, the attorneys and staff of Robbins LLP have been dedicated to helping shareholders recover losses, improve corporate governance structures, and hold company executives accountable for their wrongdoing since 2002.

To be notified if a class action against SoundHound AI, Inc. settles or to receive free alerts when corporate executives engage in wrongdoing, sign up for Stock Watch today.

data.title: Penguin Solutions Reports Q2 Fiscal 2025 Financial Results
data.description:
Penguin Solutions Inc :

  • PENGUIN SOLUTIONS REPORTS Q2 FISCAL 2025 FINANCIAL RESULTS

  • QTRLY NET SALES OF $366 MILLION

  • QTRLY NON-GAAP DILUTED EPS OF $0.52

  • OUTLOOK FY ADJUSTED EPS $1.60 +/- $0.10

  • OUTLOOK 2025 NET SALES 17% YOY GROWTH +/- 3%

  • Q2 EARNINGS PER SHARE VIEW $0.38, REVENUE VIEW $345.1 MILLION — LSEG IBES DATA

  • FY2025 EARNINGS PER SHARE VIEW $1.58, REVENUE VIEW $1.36 BILLION — LSEG IBES DATA

data.title: ANZ Confirms Enforceable Undertaking With APRA
data.description:
ANZ Group Holdings Ltd :

  • CONFIRMS ENFORCEABLE UNDERTAKING WITH APRA

  • TAKING IMMEDIATE ACTIONS IN RESPONSE TO OLIVER WYNMAN REPORT AND EU

  • ACCEPTED ALL RECOMMENDATIONS OF INDEPENDENT CULTURE AND RISK GOVERNANCE REVIEW INTO GLOBAL MARKETS BUSINESS

  • ACTIONS INCLUDE APPOINTMENT OF SENIOR EXECS TO STRENGTHEN NON-FINANCIAL RISK MANAGEMENT PRACTICES, OTHERS

data.title: RH 4Q Revenue Misses Guidance, Looks to Offset Reciprocal Tariffs
data.description:
By Sabela Ojea

RH reported fourth-quarter revenue that missed both guidance and Wall Street expectations, and said it will work to mitigate the impact of higher tariffs on imports.

The Corte Madera, Calif. furniture retailer on Wednesday reported net income for the three-months ended Feb. 1 of $13.9 million, or 69 cents a share, compared with $11.4 million, or 57 cents a share, for the same period a year earlier.

Stripping out one-time items, the company's earnings per share came in at $1.58. Analysts polled by FactSet had forecast adjusted earnings of $1.92 a share.

Revenue rose 10% to $812.4 million amid a 17% increase in total demand, missing the $829.6 million forecast by Wall Street. The company had most recently guided for a rise in revenue of 18% to 20% from the same period a year earlier amid views for a rise in demand of 20% to 22%.

Shares of RH fell 14% to $214 in post-market trading following the results. Through Wednesday's close the stock is down 36% this year.

The company is in the midst of turning around the business to bring out what it called a more inspiring and disruptive RH brand while facing higher tariffs on imported goods.

For the fiscal first quarter, RH expects revenue growth of 16% and full-year growth of about 15%.

RH does not expect a negative impact on results related to previously disclosed increases in tariffs on products from China, Canada or Mexico, Chief Executive Gary Friedman said.

"As it relates to reciprocal and other tariffs that will be announced today, as we've done with prior tariffs, we will be working with our manufacturing partners to mitigate the impact to both our margins and costs to our customers," Friedman said.

Write to Sabela Ojea at sabela.ojea@wsj.com; @sabelaojeaguix
